title: Make a claim to an employment tribunal
pageTitle: Make a claim to an employment tribunal
serviceInfo: Use this service to make claims to an employment tribunal when an employer, potential employer, trade union or person at work has treated another person unlawfully.
infoTxt: During the employment tribunal process the organisations or people you're making a claim against are known as ‘respondents’.
warningTxt1:
To make a claim you need to contact Acas and get an ‘Early conciliation certificate’ from them or give us a valid reason why you do not have one.

If you do not have a certificate or a reason you need to contact Acas (opens in new tab).
saveInfo: You do not have to make your claim in one go. You can save and return to it at any time before you submit it.
warningTxt2: Claims usually have to be made within 3 months of employment ending or problems happening. If a claim is late, you must explain why. A judge will then decide what happens next.
returnToDraftOrSubmittedClaim: Return to a draft or submitted claim
contactUsHelpTxt: Call one of our Employment Tribunal customer contact centres. They cannot give you legal advice.

stepByStep.step1Title: Check you can make a claim
stepByStep.step1Body: Before making a claim you must contact Acas and get an Early Conciliation certificate, or have a valid reason why you do not have one.
stepByStep.step1Link: Contact Acas for Early Conciliation (opens in new tab)
stepByStep.step2Body: You will need your name, address, date of birth, and contact details.
stepByStep.step3Body: You will need your employer's name and address, your job title, employment start date, and pay details.
stepByStep.step4Body: You will need to tell us the type of claim you are making, what happened, and what outcome you are seeking.
stepByStep.step5Body: Check all your answers before submitting. You can save your claim and return to it at any time before you submit.
